name: Parametric spec filtering
logic: B2B buyers arrive with a specification, not a product name — they want a 12 oz double-wall cup in kraft, and they want it in seconds. A faceted filter bar that collapses a 150-SKU catalogue to the three matching products replaces the phone call to your sales desk. It's the highest-leverage move on a manufacturer catalogue: Baymard finds only ~16% of e-commerce sites filter well, yet mediocre product lists are abandoned at 67–90% versus 17–33% for optimized ones — and Gartner shows B2B buyers spend only ~17% of their time with suppliers, so the filter is doing the selling while you sleep.

evidence.title: E-Commerce Product List Usability — Report & Benchmark
evidence.note: 9-month large-scale study; only ~16% of sites offer good filtering; mediocre lists abandon at 67–90% vs 17–33% for optimized lists.

evidence.title: Gartner — The B2B Buying Journey
evidence.note: B2B buyers spend only ~17% of total buying time with any potential supplier; most discovery is self-serve.

exemplars.name: McMaster-Carr
exemplars.why: The canonical parametric catalogue — engineers narrow ~700k parts to the exact one in seconds; its information architecture is widely praised.

metricsNote: Optimized product lists cut abandonment from 67–90% to 17–33% (Baymard).
